action.hypothesis: Seven non-improving rounds indicate the current gated-centered search is fundamentally stalled, and the updated routed encoder is now the highest-value different architecture to test because it separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude without relying on the same gated monotone ranking. A clean proxy run of routed at K=128 under the best validated training regime can reveal whether this support-selection change opens a new quality path that top-k and gated did not.
action.summary: Run the routed architecture in proxy at K=128 using the current strongest training settings to test a fundamentally different support-selection mechanism.
action.self_review: This satisfies the required mode shift away from minor parameter sweeps and uses the most defensible alternative architecture currently available in the codebase. The main risk is that routed still underperforms, but that outcome would be more informative than another local gated adjustment because it tests whether the current search direction is structurally limited.
notes_to_memory: The gated-local search now appears exhausted, so this round shifts to the routed family as the most meaningful architecture alternative presently available.
Routed should now provide a genuinely different support-selection behavior from plain top-k and gated, making this proxy result informative even if it does not beat the frontier.
This run keeps the strongest validated training regime fixed so the readout focuses on the routed selection mechanism rather than confounded optimization changes.
next_hypotheses: If routed proxy FVU is competitive with or better than top-k and gated, keep the family alive and promote or stabilize it as the next main architecture path.
If routed runs cleanly but is clearly worse, conclude that this support-selection variant is not the missing ingredient and deprioritize it.
If routed quality is promising but throughput regresses, follow with a routed-specific performance pass rather than discarding the family on speed alone.
